comment_text.snp4.5:1.2: Reading yad- here and below as yadi.

root_text.snp4.5:3.3: Tasmā hi diṭṭhaṁ va sutaṁ mutaṁ vā,
root_text.snp4.5:7.1: Tassīdha diṭṭhe va sute mute vā,

translation_text.snp4.5:1.1: If, maintaining that theirs is the “ultimate” view,
translation_text.snp4.5:1.2: a personage makes it out to be highest in the world;
translation_text.snp4.5:1.3: then they declare all others are “lesser”;
translation_text.snp4.5:1.4: that’s why they’re not beyond disputes.
translation_text.snp4.5:2.1: If they see an advantage for themselves
translation_text.snp4.5:2.2: in what’s seen, heard, or thought; <j>or in precepts or vows,
translation_text.snp4.5:2.3: in that case, having adopted that one alone,
translation_text.snp4.5:2.4: they see all others as inferior.
translation_text.snp4.5:3.1: Those who are skilled say that, too, is a knot,
translation_text.snp4.5:3.2: relying on which people see others as lesser.
translation_text.snp4.5:3.3: That’s why a mendicant ought not rely
translation_text.snp4.5:3.4: on what’s seen, heard, or thought, <j>or on precepts and vows.
translation_text.snp4.5:4.1: Nor would they form a view about the world
translation_text.snp4.5:4.2: through a notion or through precepts and vows.
translation_text.snp4.5:4.3: They would never represent themselves as “equal”,
translation_text.snp4.5:5.1: What was picked up has been set down <j>and is not grasped again;
translation_text.snp4.5:5.2: they form no dependency even on notions.
translation_text.snp4.5:5.3: They follow no side among the factions,
translation_text.snp4.5:5.4: and believe in no view at all.
translation_text.snp4.5:6.1: One here who has no wish for either end—
translation_text.snp4.5:6.2: for any state of existence in this life or the next—
translation_text.snp4.5:6.3: has adopted no dogma at all
translation_text.snp4.5:7.1: For them not even the tiniest idea is formulated here
translation_text.snp4.5:7.2: regarding what is seen, heard, or thought.
translation_text.snp4.5:7.3: That brahmin does not grasp any view—
translation_text.snp4.5:7.4: how could anyone in this world judge them?
translation_text.snp4.5:8.1: They don’t make things up or promote them,
translation_text.snp4.5:8.2: and don’t subscribe to any of the doctrines.
translation_text.snp4.5:8.3: The brahmin has no need to be led by precept or vow;
translation_text.snp4.5:8.4: gone to the far shore, one such does not return.

reference_text.snp4.5:8.1: cck25.400, dr25.535, ms18Sn_975, pts-vp-pli158, vnp803, vns810
